_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - affected limb

claim.label: Post-op X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected limb

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post Procedure X Ray show the Implant/Wire/Plate and type of Fracture?

common_queries: Post Procedure X-Ray does not show the implant/wire/plate or the type of fracture is not discernible
Pre-operative X-ray is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, and affected side
Post-operative X-ray is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, and affected side
Invoice or barcode of the implant is missing
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing

clinical_key_pointers: Tension band wiring is a fixation technique which results in absolute stability.
Interfragmentary compression and direct bone healing is obtained.
Tension band wiring (TBW) remains the most widespread method for fracture osteosynthesis, of Olecranon fractures, Patella fracture, elbow, Femur.
It is the most common operative technique for the internal fixation of olecranon fractures.
The principle of tension band wiring is to bring interfragmentary compression on a bending fracture by applying a 'tension band' on the 'tension side' of the bone.
The 'tension band' may be wire or plate.
